channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Profile of Jules Ari: YouTuber and Social Media Creator
item.description: Jules Ari is a Kenyan favourite online personality, though originally from the United States, born on May 22, 1999. She has made a name for herself since starting her YouTube channel in 2022, quickly ...

item.title: Madlanga Commission Continues Hearings on Allegations in Criminal Justice System
item.description: The Madlanga Commission of Inquiry, chaired by retired Justice Mbuyiseli Madlanga, resumed proceedings in Pretoria on Thursday, focusing on allegations of criminality, political interference, and corr...

item.title: Zach Merrett’s Attempted Trade to Hawthorn: How Essendon Blocked the Move
item.description: Essendon captain Zach Merrett explored a move to Hawthorn after growing frustrated with Essendon’s lack of progress. Initially, Merrett and his wife Alexandra, along with his manager Tom Petroro, met ...

item.title: Philippi Murder Suspects Opt Out of Bail Ahead of Court Appearance
item.description: In Cape Town, the legal proceedings for the deadly shooting at a shebeen in Philippi's Marikana informal settlement continue to unfold. On 17 January, armed individuals entered the local shebeen, robb...

item.title: Iran's Supreme Leader Signals Strait of Hormuz Will Remain Closed Amid Rising Oil Prices
item.description: Iran’s newly appointed supreme leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, has declared that the Strait of Hormuz, a crucial global shipping route for oil, will stay closed amid ongoing attacks on oil tankers. This ann...

item.title: Kwara APC Crisis: Governor Allegedly Orders Police Arrests Amid Protests Over Manipulated Ward Congress Executive Lists
item.description: More than 50 members of the All Progressives Congress (APC) for Kwara State don dey face arrest after Governor AbdulRahman AbdulRazaq reportedly tell Nigeria Police Force make dem clamp down on dem. T...

item.title: NEMA Introduces Digital Platform to Track Industrial Pollution Across Kenya
item.description: Nairobi — The National Environment Management Authority (NEMA) in partnership with United Platform Solutions has rolled out a new digital system called the Kenya Online Continuous Emissions Monitoring...

item.title: Concerns Raised Over Lack of Public Review of LA28 Human Rights Plan
item.description: More than two months after the human rights report for the 2028 Los Angeles Olympics was submitted, the city has yet to make it publicly available, raising concerns among advocates about homelessness ...

item.title: African Consumers Shift to Complex and Local Flavours in Food and Drinks
item.description: According to the 2026 Taste Charts by Kerry Group, African consumers dey move away from the old simple flavours and dey look for more layered, unique taste experiences for their food and drinks. The r...

item.title: Twelve Nigerian Lawmakers Switch Parties in Major Political Realignment
item.description: Today, Thursday 12th March 2026, the Nigerian House of Representatives experience serious political movement as twelve lawmakers defect from their original parties. Six members moved from the Peoples ...

item.title: Starmer faces criticism over Mandelson appointment amid media scrutiny
item.description: Senior ministers in the UK government were visibly uneasy following the controversial appointment of Peter Mandelson, with Cabinet Office minister Nick Thomas-Symonds unexpectedly tasked to face a cha...

item.title: Waterloo installs fences and declares community safety event to prevent unsanctioned St. Patrick's Day gatherings
item.description: In preparation for the upcoming St. Patrick's Day weekend, the City of Waterloo has taken proactive steps to maintain public order in its university district. Metal fences have been installed along ke...

item.title: NMG share rises sharply after announcement of major stake sale
item.description: Nation Media Group’s (NMG) share price at the Nairobi Securities Exchange has recorded a sharp rally over just two trading sessions after investors reacted to news that a significant stake in the comp...

item.title: Christina Frasco reassigned; Verna Buensuceso takes over as acting Tourism chief
item.description: MANILA, Philippines – Christina Garcia-Frasco has officially stepped down as Secretary of the Department of Tourism (DOT), Malacañang confirmed on March 12, 2026. She has been appointed as the Preside...

item.title: Former Antique Governor and Officials Face Graft Charges Over P531 Million Government Center Project
item.description: Former Antique governor Rhodora Cadiao, together with eight other provincial officials, has been charged with graft in connection to the P531.2 million construction of the Antique Government Center. T...

item.title: Minister Urges Tanzanian Universities to Develop Local Textbooks for Relevant Education
item.description: The Tanzanian Government, through the Minister for Education, has urged universities to start producing their own textbooks instead of depending on foreign ones mainly from the US and Europe. This cal...

item.title: PM Modi Assures Energy Stability Amid LPG Supply Concerns
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi, addressing the NXT Summit 2026 in Delhi, strongly criticised opposition parties who are creating panic about LPG shortages, stating that they are 'exposing themselves.' H...

item.title: Universities urged to champion gender equality through policies and support for women
item.description: Universities in Tanzania and beyond are being called upon to go further than just teaching and doing research; they must take the lead in promoting gender equality and influencing the whole society. T...

item.title: Israeli Military Withdraws Charges Against Five Soldiers in Sde Teiman Abuse Case
item.description: In a controversial move that has drawn global attention, the Israeli military announced on Thursday that it has dropped all charges against five soldiers previously accused of sexually assaulting a Pa...

item.title: India Reviews Fuel Supply Requests from Neighbouring Countries Amid Energy Crisis
item.description: India is currently reviewing requests for fuel supply from Bangladesh, Maldives, and Sri Lanka in light of the ongoing energy crisis triggered by the West Asia conflict. The External Affairs Ministry ...
